record.field: odoo_record_retriever
odoo_record_retriever

Use `odoo_record_retriever` to retrieve records from any Odoo model. This tool allows you to fetch data from the database by specifying the model name, domain filters, fields to retrieve, and a limit on the number of records returned.
USAGE GUIDELINES: - For searching people/contacts by name: Use model='res.partner' - For all records always include the field "id", may be useful for later requests. - For contacts, always include fields like ["name", "display_name", "phone", "mobile", "email"] - Use 'ilike' operator for partial name matching (e.g., "Carlos" will match "Carlos Perez") - Use '=' operator only for exact matches - Always set appropriate limits (10-20 records max for person searches) - The domain parameter is a python array/list. NEVER pass a string.
EXAMPLES: - Find person by name: model="res.partner", domain=[["name", "ilike", "John"]], fields=["name", "phone", "mobile", "email"] - Find active contacts: model="res.partner", domain=[["active", "=", true], ["is_company", "=", False]] - Find contacts with phone: model="res.partner", domain=[["phone", "!=", False]]
Search and retrieve contacts, companies, and other records from your Odoo database. I can help you find people by name, phone number, email, or other criteria.
odoo_record_creator
odoo_record_creator
Create new contacts, companies, or other records in your Odoo system. I can help you add new information to your database.
odoo_record_updater
odoo_record_updater

Use `odoo_record_updater` to update existing records in any Odoo model. This tool allows you to modify data in the database by specifying the model name, domain filters to identify records, and a dictionary of field values to update.
SAFETY FEATURES: - Defaults to updating only 1 record at a time (use limit parameter to change) - Returns count of updated records and their IDs for verification - Requires user consent before execution due to data modification capabilities
USAGE GUIDELINES: - Always use specific domain filters to target the correct records - Test with limit=1 first when uncertain about the domain scope - Use the odoo_record_retriever tool first to verify which records will be affected - The domain parameter is a python array/list. NEVER pass a string. - Use exact ID matching when updating a specific record: [["id", "=", record_id]]
EXAMPLES: - Update contact phone of partner id 123: model="res.partner", domain=[["id", "=", 123]], values={"phone": "+1234567890"} - Update all contacts named John: model="res.partner", domain=[["name", "ilike", "John"]], values={"mobile": "+9876543210"}, limit=5 - Activate all inactive records: domain=[["active", "=", False]], values={"active": True} - Update company with id 456, set its website to "https://example.com": domain=[["id", "=", 456], ["is_company", "=", true]], values={"website": "https://example.com"}
PARAMETERS: - model: The Odoo model technical name (e.g., "res.partner", "sale.order") - domain: List of filters to identify records to update - values: Dictionary of field names and their new values - limit: Maximum number of records to update (default: 1 for safety)
Update and modify existing contacts, companies, or other records in your Odoo system. I can help you change information in your database.
odoo_record_unlinker
odoo_record_unlinker

Delete existing records from any Odoo model. This tool allows you to remove data from the database by specifying the model name and domain filters to identify records to delete. For safety, it defaults to deleting only one record at a time. Due to its data modification capabilities, this tool requires user consent before execution.
Delete contacts, companies, or other records from your Odoo system. I can help you remove information from your database (requires your confirmation).
odoo_model_method_executor
odoo_model_method_executor

**VERY HIGH RISK TOOL:** Executes a specified method on an Odoo model or specific records. Provide the model's technical name, the method name, and optionally a list of record IDs, positional arguments ('args'), and keyword arguments ('kwargs'). If record_ids is null/empty, the method is called on the model itself (e.g., for `search`, `create`, `@api.model`, `@staticmethod`). **WARNING:** This tool can modify or delete data, trigger complex actions, or cause system errors if used improperly. It bypasses standard UI workflows. **IMPORTANT:** The LLM using this tool MUST explicitly ask the user for confirmation before EVERY execution, clearly stating the model, method, records (if any), and arguments being used. Private methods (starting with '_') are disallowed by default unless 'allow_private' is explicitly set to true (use with extreme caution). Returns a JSON representation of the method's result or an error message.
Execute advanced operations and custom methods on your Odoo data. This is a powerful tool for complex tasks (requires your explicit confirmation before each use).
odoo_model_inspector
odoo_model_inspector

Comprehensive inspection of an Odoo model providing a complete analysis of its structure. This tool retrieves detailed information about:
1. **Basic Model Data**: Name, description, module, and inheritance structure. 2. **Fields Analysis**: Types, properties, relations, required/readonly status, and help text. 3. **Methods Inspection**: Signatures, docstrings, decorators (@api.model, @api.depends, etc.), and method types.
Use this tool to understand any Odoo model's structure, fields, and available methods. You can filter fields and methods by name or type, limit the number returned, and include/exclude private elements (those starting with '_').
This information is essential for: - Understanding available data structures - Determining which fields to use for record creation/updates - Finding appropriate methods to execute with the model_method_executor tool - Building domain filters for record retrieval
Perfect for exploring unfamiliar models before performing operations on them.
Analyze and explore the structure of your Odoo data models. I can help you understand what information is available and how it's organized.
user_greeting
user_greeting

Use `user_greeting` tool to greet/salute back users and provide information about available tools for the current thread/assistant.
This tool is designed to: 1. **Welcome Users**: Provide personalized greetings based on interaction type (initial, help, capabilities) 2. **Tool Discovery**: Query the database to find and list all available tools for the current thread
**IMPORTANT Instructions:** - Use the `user_greeting` tool for greetings. Do not use tools for follow-ups or casual messages. - When responding to this tool's output, you MUST ALWAYS format your response using proper Markdown: - Use # for main headings - Use ## for subheadings - Use **bold** for emphasis - Use bullet points with - or * - Use numbered lists with 1. 2. 3. - Use `code` for tool names - Use > for callouts or tips
**Parameters:** - greeting_type: 'initial' (default), or 'capabilities' - thread_id: Automatically injected by the framework (shows thread-specific tools)
**Use Cases:** - Answering users initial salutation - Listing available capabilities for the current thread/assistant
**Examples** - Hola, buen dia, como estas?: greeting_type: 'initial' - ¿Que puedes hacer?: greeting_type: 'capabilities'
This tool is completely read-only and safe to use, providing only informational content to enhance user experience.
Welcome you and show you what I can help you with. This helps you get started and understand my capabilities.
